query: What is the structure of the EuroQol 5-Dimension Health Assessment (EQ-5D)?

retrieved_docs.title: Hand and Wrist Rheumatoid Arthritis -- Evaluation -- Patient-Reported Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Health Assessment Questionnaire (HAQ) : Physical function assessment is one of the most important tools that correlate well with long term prognosis in RA. This includes multiple questions about patients’ ability/inability to perform activities of daily living. Multiple tools have been proposed for physical function assessment, including Arthritis Impact Measurement Scale (AIMS), clinical HAQ (CLINHAQ), Euro-QoL 5-D (EQ-5D), and several modifications have been made to the HAQ tool. The modified HAQ (HAQ-II) is the most widely used tool.

retrieved_docs.title: Quality Of Life -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: As alluded to in the previous section, the primary concern with the concept of QoL is a lack of a uniform definition. Compared to financial measures or those that can be otherwise measured quantitatively, such as gross domestic product, QoL remains elusive regarding an exact means of measurement across world cultures, regions, and demographics. Many academic circles have pushed to break QoL into smaller components for more accurate and meaningful evaluation. One example is dividing the concept into domains (engaged theory), while the other approach divides QoL into personal well-being and life evaluation. This divide remains a widely debated topic in the literature. [5] [2] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Quality of Well-being Scale -- Summary / Explanation -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Health-related quality of life is a term used to encapsulate a full picture of how one’s health impacts their overall well-being. With regard to health-related quality of life, the standard measurement in medical cost-effectiveness research is the quality-adjusted life year. [2] The quality-adjusted life year incorporates health-related quality of life in addition to one’s entire life to create a single all-inclusive expression of health outcomes. The quality of well-being scale was the first assessment tool specifically created to measure the quality of life to estimate quality-adjusted life years. [1] By using the quality of well-being scale before and after treatments in well-controlled studies, researchers can see how well treatments work and whether one’s quality of life improves as a result. Tools such as this can assist healthcare professionals in making informed treatment decisions. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Secondary Causes of Obesity and Comprehensive Diagnostic Evaluation -- Function -- Quality of Life Assessment
retrieved_docs.content: Health-related quality of life assessment provides important outcomes data for treatment planning and monitoring therapeutic response. [16] Validated quality of life assessment tools include: Impact of Weight on Quality of Life-Lite Short Form-36 Health Survey Beck depression inventory

retrieved_docs.title: Quality Of Life -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The World Health Organization explains QoL as a subjective evaluation of one's perception of reality relative to one's goals as observed through the lens of one's culture and value system. The Quality of Life Research Unit at the University of Toronto defines QoL as how much a person can enjoy the valued possibilities of their lives. It is essential to recognize the difference between QoL and other somewhat similar concepts that might get easily confused in the literature, such as the standard of living and health-related quality of life. QoL differs from the former in that the standard of living is largely based on economic status and income.

retrieved_docs.title: Quality Of Life -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: QoL differs from the public health measure of health-related quality of life in that the latter is a measure that explores the connection between health and QoL. As there has been recognized difficulty with arriving at a universally accepted definition and measurement, recent studies have attempted to reframe QoL into separate domains. One example of this reframing is the "engaged theory," which takes QoL and divides it into 4 main domains: economics, culture, politics, and ecology. [1] [2] [3] [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Quality of Well-being Scale -- Summary / Explanation -- Self-Administered Version
retrieved_docs.content: The Quality of Well-Being Scale Self-Administered version takes approximately ten minutes to complete and does not require a trainer interviewer. [19] This version asks patients about symptoms and functioning over the past three days. The purpose behind this timeframe is to reduce recall bias without reducing the scale’s ability to measure over a period of time. The Quality of Well-Being Scale Interviewer-Administered version asks patients about the past six days. While the self-administered version assesses over half the amount of time (prior 3 days), there was no significant difference in scores, suggesting strong concordance validity. [19] The Quality of Well-Being Scale Self-Administered version covers many symptoms and asks patients about various aspects of health, including signs and predictors of numerous disease conditions and their associated severity. When creating the self-administered version, several focus groups of physicians assisted in the creation of this tool, and as such, this assessment closely resembles how a healthcare professional would conduct an examination (interview and assessment) of a patient’s symptoms, adding to its clinical utility. To further increase its clinical utility, the Quality of Well-Being Scale Self-Administered version has been translated into Swedish, Dutch, Spanish, French-Canadian, German, Chinese, and Italian.

retrieved_docs.title: Common Measures of Disability -- Function -- Self-Report Measures
retrieved_docs.content: Pain assessment tools or questionnaires and self-report measures have a key role in evaluating the impact of various health conditions on a patient's function and well-being. Commonly used tools include: The McGill Pain Questionnaire and the Pain Disability Index, which are designed to assess pain intensity and its impact on daily functioning. The Patient Health Questionnaire-9 and Generalized Anxiety Disorders Assessment, which assess self-reported symptoms and functional impairments in individuals with depression or anxiety. Please see StatPearls' companion resource, " Disability Evaluation ," for more information. [41] The Quality of Life Inventory, which rates perceived quality of life across different domains for individuals with multiple sclerosis and HIV or AIDS. [42]

retrieved_docs.title: Quality of Well-being Scale -- Summary / Explanation -- Patient Populations
retrieved_docs.content: Many studies have been conducted using the Quality of Well-Being Scale on a variety of patient populations. Notable examples include patients with: Emphysema [3] Osteoarthritis [4] [5] [6] Musculoskeletal disease [7] Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease [8] Alzheimer disease [9] Panic disorder [10] Epilepsy [11] AIDS [6] Cystic fibrosis [12] Atrial fibrillation [13] Depression [14] [5] Limb amputations [15] High-risk pregnancy [16] Fibromyalgia [5] Diabetes [17] Headaches [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Quality of Well-being Scale -- Summary / Explanation -- Modes of Administration
retrieved_docs.content: The caliber of the Quality of Well-Being Scale has been somewhat limited due to its length, complexity, branching questions, and the need for a trained interviewer for administration. In response to these limiting factors and in an effort to increase utility, the scale expanded its modes of administration to include four options for administration: self-administered, interviewer-administered, proxy-administered, and online.

retrieved_docs.title: Pain Assessment -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: The Pain, Enjoyment, General Activity (PEG) tool supports multidimensional assessment and emphasizes function and quality of life. [29] A patient with chronic pain reporting daily 7/10 pain may undergo treatment, both pharmacologic and nonpharmacologic, that reduces their pain to 5/10. The numerical change may appear small, but improvements in enjoyment, daily function, and life satisfaction suggest substantial progress. PEG assigns scores from 0 to 10 in each domain. Higher scores indicate worse function and less-controlled pain. This tool offers a quick, structured, and repeatable measure for tracking a patient’s pain and response to treatment over time. [30] Clinicians can apply PEG across care settings.

retrieved_docs.title: Common Measures of Disability -- Function -- Self-Report Measures
retrieved_docs.content: The Short Form 36 Physical Functioning Scale evaluates a patient's perceptions regarding their general health in addition to their physical, social, and emotional functioning. [38] Clinicians who frequently treat specific conditions should consider using validated questionnaires as part of routine care. Common orthopedic measures include the Disability of the Shoulder, Arm, and Hand (DASH) Questionnaire, the American Academy of Orthopedic Surgeons Lower Limb Questionnaire, and the Lower-Limb Tasks Questionnaire. [39]

retrieved_docs.title: Common Measures of Disability -- Function -- Self-Report Measures
retrieved_docs.content: Self-report measures are tools used to collect information directly from individuals about their experiences, symptoms, behaviors, or perceptions related to their health or well-being. These measures rely on individuals' self-assessments to report specific aspects of their condition or functioning, providing valuable insights into how they perceive and experience various health-related factors. Numerous condition-specific questionnaires exist. For example, healthcare professionals often use up to 10 different low back pain questionnaires to assess functional limitations associated with the condition. The Oswestry Disability Questionnaire, for instance, evaluates pain and complex activities. Please see StatPearls' companion resource, " Seating And Wheelchair Evaluation ," for more information.

retrieved_docs.title: Utah Controlled Substance Prescribing -- History and Physical -- History
retrieved_docs.content: A multidimensional assessment of a patient's pain and the severity of their pain can be completed. A p ain, e njoyment, g eneral Activity (PEG) tool can aid the multidimensional assessment of patients in pain. [30] The PEG score focuses on function and quality of life. A chronic pain patient who experiences daily 7/10 pain is treated with both pharmacological and nonpharmacological therapies. Following treatment, their pain is 5/10. A few points might not seem like a significant difference, but if their enjoyment and quality of life and function are improving, treatment may have had a profound impact on the patient's life. The PEG tool is scored 0 to 10 for each category. The higher the score, the worse the function and uncontrolled pain.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2635
retrieved_docs.content: Internal fixation of fracture (تثبيت داخلي للكسر), hip joint surgery (جراحة مفصل الورك), femoral head replacement (إعاضة رأس الفخذ), and multiple fractures (كسور متعددة) are all identified as members of the clinical category osteoporosis/low bone density (هشاشة العظام/قلة العظام (SMQ)). This classification highlights that patients with osteoporosis or reduced bone mass frequently require these orthopedic interventions, either because the weakened skeletal structure predisposes them to fractures that need internal fixation, leads to hip joint pathology necessitating surgical repair, may result in femoral head degeneration requiring replacement, or manifests as multiple concurrent fractures. Recognizing these procedures as integral components of osteoporosis management underscores the importance of comprehensive assessment and coordinated care for individuals with low bone density.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 4 -- Community 2827
retrieved_docs.content: Drugs used in erectile dysfunction include tadalafil and udenafil, both of which are members of this therapeutic class and function as phosphodiesterase type 5 inhibitors to improve erectile function in affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 2 -- Community 1085
retrieved_docs.content: Internal fixation of bone without fracture reduction is a specific form of internal fixation of fracture (تثبيت داخلي للكسر) and is employed as a treatment for fractures or dislocations of the hip and femur, for other lower‑extremity fractures (excluding the hip or femur), and for fractures of the radius and ulna. This procedure, together with other fracture‑related interventions such as hip‑head prosthetic replacement (إعاضة رأس الفخذ) and the broader category of “other fractures,” is classified within the osteoporosis/low bone mass syndrome (هشاشة العظام/قلة العظام SMQ). Consequently, these surgical treatments are particularly relevant for patients with reduced bone density, highlighting the close relationship between osteoporotic bone disease and the need for internal fixation or prosthetic reconstruction of various skeletal sites.

retrieved_docs.content: Estradiol and estrogeny are hormonal agents that may be used to treat menopausal disorders, providing therapeutic options for alleviating the symptoms associated with the menopausal transition. Both substances are forms of estrogen therapy and are considered in clinical management to improve quality of life for patients experiencing menopausal-related changes.

Together, these entities define an integrated framework of thigh‑and‑leg orthopedic care that spans growth modulation, fracture fixation, malunion correction, lengthening/shortening, prosthetic revision, soft‑tissue repair, and preventive strategies, all contextualized within the broader impact of osteoporosis on lower‑extremity bone health.

retrieved_docs.content: clavicle, femur, humerus, radius, scapula, and ulna—to the systemic disease category of osteoporosis or low bone density. “Fracture of neck of femur (hip)” itself is classified within the same osteoporosis SMQ, underscoring low bone mass as a key risk factor. Internal‑fixation procedures (identified as “تثبيت داخلي للكسر”) are associated with multiple treatment categories: “fracture or dislocation of hip and femur,” “fracture or dislocation of lower extremity (other than hip or femur),” and “fracture or dislocation of radius and ulna.” These associations emphasize that bone quality influences surgical decision‑making for both hip and peripheral limb injuries. Within the osteoporosis SMQ, additional members such as “جراحة مفصل الورك” (hip joint surgery), “إعاضة رأس الفخذ” (hip head prosthesis), and “كسور متعددة” (multiple fractures) illustrate the spectrum of interventions and clinical manifestations that arise in patients with compromised bone health. By connecting specific fracture

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 2 -- Community 1186
retrieved_docs.content: Closed fracture of intracapsular section of femur is mapped to fracture of neck of femur (hip), indicating that this specific intracapsular injury is a form of hip neck fracture. The category fracture of neck of femur (hip) classifies itself as a hip neck fracture and has هشاشة العظام/قلة العظام (SMQ) (osteoporosis/low bone density) as a member, reflecting the strong association between reduced bone quality and neck‑of‑femur fractures. Within the هشاشة العظام/قلة العظام (SMQ) grouping, إعانة رأس الفخذ (hip‑head support) and كسور متعددة (multiple fractures) are listed as members, showing that bone‑density disorders encompass both therapeutic interventions such as hip‑head support and clinical presentations like multiple fractures. Together, these relationships illustrate how a closed intracapsular femur fracture fits into the broader classification of hip neck fractures, how osteoporosis/low bone density underlies these injuries, and how related entities such as hip‑head support procedures and the occurrence of multiple fractures are integrated within the same disease framework, informing comprehensive patient evaluation and care.

This indicates that these surgical interventions are specifically associated with the management of patients who have osteoporosis or reduced bone density, reflecting their role in treating fractures and hip pathology that result from weakened bone structure.

Open reduction of these closed fractures—whether performed with external or internal skeletal fixation—is listed as a child procedure within the “SHOULDER AND ARM: SURGICAL REPAIRS, CLOSURES AND RECONSTRUCTIONS” domain, illustrating a unified surgical classification for upper‑extremity and selected lower‑extremity injuries. Internal fixation of bone without fracture reduction is likewise a member of the osteoporosis/low bone‑mass group and is linked to multiple fracture‑treatment categories. Hip‑head prosthetic replacement (إعاضة رأس الفخذ) is also categorized within the osteoporosis/low bone‑mass group, highlighting the importance of bone quality in joint arthroplasty.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 2 -- Community 499
retrieved_docs.content: Closed fracture of intracapsular section of femur is mapped to the clinical entity fracture of neck of femur (hip). The fracture of neck of femur (hip) is closely linked with هشاشة العظام/قلة العظام (SMQ), indicating that osteoporosis or low bone density is a common underlying condition predisposing patients to this type of hip fracture. In the context of هشاشة العظام/قلة العظام (SMQ), therapeutic procedures such as تثبيت داخلي للكسر (internal fixation of the fracture) and إعاضة رأس الفخذ (hip head replacement) are considered appropriate management options. These interventions are employed to stabilize the fracture and restore hip function in patients whose bone quality is compromised by osteoporosis or low bone density.
